title: Standard protocols for detection of human CMV-specific T cells
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ix72q","text":"This document describes protocols used for in vitro restimulation of cryopreserved PBMC with CMV lysates and subsequent detection of CMV-specific CD4+ and CD8+ T cells by intracellular staining of cytokines.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":43,"length":8},{"style":"sup","offset":149,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":158,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"sogke","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"g56jj","text":"This protocol describes detection CMV-reactive T cells in human PBMC. Briefly, PBMC are stimulated with CMV lysate for 18 hours. Afterwards, CMV-reactive CD4+ and CD8+ T cells are detected by intracellular staining of cytokines, including IFN-γ. Our group has applied this method to frozen PBMC samples from patients with advanced melanoma.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":157,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":166,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b9yop","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"lemin","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Add 20 ml FCS to 30 ml RPMI 1640
Freezing medium 1 can be stored up to 1 month at 4°C
Add 10 ml DMSO to 40 ml FCS
Freezing medium 2 can be stored up to 1 month at 4°C
Add 9.3 ml of RPMI 1640 into a falcon tube.
Add 0.5 ml of human AB serum (HABS)
Add 0.1 ml of GlutaMax
Add 0.1 ml of Pen-Strep
Pre-cool cryotubes, freezing container and all reagents to 4°C,
Work on ice using cold reagents in the laminar flow hood
Add 1 ml Freezing Medium 1 to up to 20 x 10 6 pelleted PBMC
Incubate 20 min on ice
Slowly add 1 ml cold Freezing Medium 2
Mix by gently pipetting
Transfer 1 ml of cell suspension (10 x 10 6 cells/ml) per cryotube
Place cryotubes in a freezing container and store at -80°C
After 1 day, remove cryotubes from freezing container and store them at -80°C for up to 6 months
Alternatively, samples can be stored in liquid nitrogen for longer than 6 months
Pre-warm water bath to 37°C
Thaw cells quickly by swirling the cryotube in the water bath (<60 seconds)
Before last ice crystal melts, place cryotube in a cold rack
Transfer the content of the cryotube into a falcon tube
Add 10 ml of cold PBS containing 10% human AB serum dropwise
Count cells
Centrifuge at 200 g for 10 min
Aspirate supernatant using vacuum pump
Resuspend cell pellet in culture medium at up to 10 x 10 6 /ml
Add 150 µl of cell suspension per well in a 96-well plate
Incubate for 6 hours at 37°C 5% CO 2 before proceeding with stimulation
Add 5 µg/ml CMV lysate to each well.
Add 1µg/ml αCD28 and αCD49d to each well.
As a negative control, leave one unstimulated well
Optionally, add 5µg/ml αPD1, αCTLA-4 or isotype control to the corresponding wells
Incubate for 18 hours at 37°C 5% CO 2
In the last 4 hours of incubation, add 2 µl/ml Protein Transport Inhibitor Cocktail to all the wells (0.3 µl 500X stock per well containing 150 µl medium)
As a positive control, add 10 µg/ml SEB for 18 hours or alternatively 2 µl/ml Cell Stimulation Cocktail to one well for the last 4 hours.
Transfer cells from culture wells into a polypropylene V bottom 96 well plate
Centrifuge at 1400rpm for 3 min
Resuspend pellet in 20 µl PBS containing ViaKrome Fixable Viability Dye 808 (32,5µl dye/ml)
Incubate for 20 min at RT in the dark
Wash samples with 180 µl Cell Staining Buffer (CSB) by centrifugation at 1400 rpm for 3 min
Aspirate supernatant using vacuum pump
Add 10 µl 10% FcR block in CSB and shake at 800 rpm for 60'' in a rotary shake
Incubate at 4 °C for 15 min in the dark
Add 20 µl surface antibodies mastermix as indicated in Figure 1
Shake at 800 rpm for 5 min and incubate for further 25 min at 4°C in the dark
Wash samples with 170 µl CSB by centrifugation at 1400 rpm for 3 min
Aspirate supernatant using vacuum pump
Add 40 µl CSB to each well and resuspend cell pellets
Add 50 µl IC Fixation Buffer
Incubate for 30 min at RT in the dark
Wash samples with 110 µl Perm Buffer by centrifugation at 1400 rpm for 3 min
Aspirate supernatant using vacuum pump
Add 40 µl intracellular antibody mastermix as indicated in Figure 1.
Shake at 800 rpm for 5 min and incubate for further 25 min at RT in the dark
Wash samples with 160 µl Perm Buffer by centrifugation at 1400 rpm for 3 min
Aspirate supernatant using vacuum pump
Wash samples with 200 µl Perm Buffer by centrifugation at 1400 rpm for 3 min
Aspirate supernatant using vacuum pump
Resuspend in 150 µl CSB for analysis by flow cytometry

Our gating strategy for analysis of IFN-γ-producing T cells is shown in Figure 2. Samples were acquired using a Cytoflex LX cytometer (Beckman Coulter) and analysed with Kaluza analysis software v.2.1
The authors declare no competing financial interests
Figure 1. Flow cytometry panel for detecting CMV-reactive T cells Figure 2. Gating strategy for analysis of IFN-γ-producing T cells
